q: Plants lack a (A. respiratory B. nervous C. transport D. reproductive) system like animals. (2025)
The (A. environmental B. functional C. evolutionary D. anatomical) stimuli to which plants respond to abiotic or biotic stress. (2025)
The cells of (A. vascular bundle B. meristems C. pericycle D. cortex) are isodiametric, thin wall, and consist of large nucleus and dense cytoplasm. (2025)
During development, structures and functions of organs or cells are (A. changed B. unchanged C. increased D. decreased). (2025)
The process of development occurs in an ordered fashion within a single diploid cell called (A. embryo, B. zygote, C. meristem, D. gamete). (2024)
Gibberellins are plant hormones that include (A. indole acetic acid B. abscisic acid C. adenine D. gibberellic acid). (2026)
A plant hormone is an (A. organic B. inorganic C. nitrogen D. ammonia) compound. (2025)
(A. Cytokinin B. Abscisic acid C. Ethylene D. Auxin) is an example in the group of hormones that promote dormancy in plants. (2025)
(A. Abscisic acid B. Cytokinin C. Gibberellin D. Ethylene) stimulates ripening of fruits. (2025)
Phytohormones are essential (A. organic, B. inorganic, C. chemical, D. hydrogen) compounds required in very small quantities. (2024)
Coconut milk is a rich source of (A. gibberellins, B. auxins, C. ethylenes, D. cytokinins). (2025)
The only gaseous phytohormone is (A. ethylene, B. gibberellins, C. cytokinin, D. abscisic acid). (2024)
The dormancy of seeds can be broken by (A. stratification, B. hormonal treatment, C. scarification, D. all the above). (2024)
The vivipary germination is found in (A. coconut B. mango C. agave D. castor). (2025)
Effect or requirements of relative length of day and night on flowering is called (A. photomorphogenesis B. photoperiodism C. vernalization D. skotomorphogenesis). (2026)
Flowering hormone is suggested to be (A. auxin B. gibberellin C. ethylene D. florigen). (2025)
Phytochrome, cryptochrome and phototropin are called (A. photomorphogenesis B. photoreceptor C. phototropism D. photoperiodism). (2025)
(A. Light, B. Water, C. Temperature, D. Gravity) causes a variety of responses in the plant body in addition to photosynthesis. (2024)
The central nervous system is composed of the brain and (A. spinal nerves B. cranial nerves C. spinal cord D. pons). (2025)
The hindbrain consists of the (A. thalamus B. cerebellum C. skull D. cerebrum), pons and medulla oblongata. (2025)
The skull forms a protective bony armour around the (A. face B. eyes C. head D. brain). (2025)
The largest part of the brain is (A. cerebellum, B. thalamus, C. cerebrum, D. hypothalamus). (2024 / 2025)
The pons is located above and in front of the (A. medulla oblongata, B. cerebellum, C. hypothalamus D. midbrain) in the brainstem. (2024)
The midbrain is located above the (A. cerebellum B. medulla oblongata C. hypothalamus D. pons) in the brainstem. (2025)
The brain can be subdivided into (A. one, B. three, C. four, D. five) general regions. (2024)
The parasympathetic system uses the neurotransmitter (A. norepinephrine, B. endorphins, C. dopamine, D. acetylcholine) to control organ responses. (2024)
Injury to the spinal column can also damage the (A. brain, B. spinal cord, C. medulla, D. cerebellum). (2024)
The nervous system is composed of only (A. one B. two C. four D. five) main type of cells. (2026)
The activity of neurones is supported by another type of cells called (A. glial cells B. axon C. dendron D. myelin sheath). (2026)
The activity of (A. neurones B. neurons C. nephrons D. axon) is supported by another type of cells called glial cells. (2025)
(A. Interneurones. B. Motor C. Sensory D. Bipolar) neurones transmit impulses from the sensory receptors to the central nervous system. (2025)
Multipolar neurone found in the (A. brain B. muscles C. arteries D. veins) and spinal cord. (2025)
Inadequate production of (A. dopamine B. serotonin C. norepinephrine D. endorphins) is linked to Alzheimer's disease. (2026)
A neuromuscular junction is a synapse between a motor neurone and a (A. muscle B. nerve C. glial D. neuroglial) cell. (2025)
The middle coat of the eyeball is (A. sclera B. cornea C. choroid D. retina). (2025)
The endocrine glands and their hormones make up the (A. nervous system, B. circulatory system, C. excretory system, D. endocrine system). (2024)
(A. Calcitonin B. Insulin C. Estrogen D. Prolactin) hormone is produced by the thyroid gland. (2025)
(A. Estrogen B. Oxytocin C. Testosterone D. Progesterone) hormone promotes sperm formation. (2025)
(A. Testosterone B. Estrogen C. Insulin D. Calcitonin) hormone promotes sperm formation and development of the male sexual characteristics. (2025)
